task: C26 Task 14 — Wan + ComfyUI 2-CLI cold-skip / PROVEN-PROTECTION
killed_by: operator (background task interrupted ~8 min into gen1 after user observed stall)

diagnosis.symptom: Pod in container-restart loop. Every util tick reads uptime_seconds=1 — the container is restarting faster than the heartbeat_interval_s=30 cadence. Both axes (gpu_util_percent=0, cpu_percent=13) are below their stall thresholds (5/20), so the counter SHOULD increment, but _update_counter's uptime-decrease guard fires on every tick (prev_uptime 1+ → curr_uptime 1) and resets the counter to 0. After 480 s of unbroken low util, counter is still 0.
diagnosis.design_hole: _update_counter's restart-blip guard was designed for 'container migration / spot reclaim' — a single restart event that should NOT count as workload stall. It was not designed for chronic restart loops where 'restart' is the stall itself. STALL_REAP never fires because the counter never accumulates.
diagnosis.not_c25: C25 wire fix (preserve-and-merge dockerArgs) is orthogonal and verified — heartbeats tick, pod stays in ledger LIVE.
diagnosis.is_c26_partial: C26 Phase A (FakeEngine + interval=10 s + steady low util) PROVEN at 76.6 s. Phase B (Wan + ComfyUI in a container restart loop) NOT PROTECTED by the shipped design.

evidence_for_acceptance: Phase A sidecar tests/live/_c26_phase_a_smoke_evidence.json — STALL_REAP fired at counter=6 (60 s of unbroken low util) ⇒ C26 substrate + classify + reaper path all work end-to-end.
Phase B ledger snapshot above — counter stuck at 0 after 480 s of low util because of the restart-loop interaction with the uptime-decrease guard in _update_counter.
Live runtime{} probe at kill confirmed gpu=0, cpu=13, uptime=1 → matches ledger snapshot.
deferred_to: C27 — restart-loop stall detection. A separate predicate (e.g. uptime_seconds < threshold_uptime for K consecutive ticks) needed alongside the existing low-util predicate. C26 ships PARTIAL: substrate + Phase-A class of stall covered; restart-loop class not covered.
